# Framework Laptop 16 - Input Module Firmware/Software

This repository contains both the firmware for the Framework Laptop 16 input modules,
as well as the tool to control them.

Rust firmware project setup based off of: https://github.com/rp-rs/rp2040-project-template

## Modules

See pages of the individual modules for details about how they work and how
they're controlled.

- [LED Matrix](ledmatrix/README.md)
- [Minimal C1 Input Module](c1minimal/README.md)
- [2nd Display](b1display/README.md)
- [QT PY RP2040](qtpy/README.md)

## Generic Features

All modules are built with an RP2040 microcontroller
Features that all modules share

- Firmware written in bare-metal Rust
- Reset into RP2040 bootloader when firmware crashes/panics
- Sleep Mode to save power
- API over USB ACM Serial Port - Requires no Drivers on Windows and Linux
  - Go to sleep
  - Reset into bootloader
  - Control and read module state (brightness, displayed image, ...)

## Control from the host

To build your own application see the: [API command documentation](commands.md)

Or use our `inputmodule-control` app, which you can download from the latest
[GH Actions](https://github.com/FrameworkComputer/inputmodule-rs/actions) run or
the [release page](https://github.com/FrameworkComputer/inputmodule-rs/releases).

For device specific commands, see their individual documentation pages.

### GUI and Python
There are also a python library and GUI tool. See their [README](python/README.md).

![](res/ledmatrixgui-home.png)

###### Permissions on Linux
To ensure that the input module's port is accessible, install the `udev` rule and trigger a reload:

```sh
sudo cp release/50-framework-inputmodule.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/
sudo udevadm control --reload && sudo udevadm trigger
```

##### Common commands:

###### Listing available devices

```sh
> inputmodule-control --list
/dev/ttyACM0
  VID     0x32AC
  PID     0x0020
  SN      FRAKDEAM0020110001
  Product LED_Matrix
/dev/ttyACM1
  VID     0x32AC
  PID     0x0021
  SN      FRAKDEAM0000000000
  Product B1_Display
```

###### Apply command to single device

By default a command will be sent to all devices that can be found, to apply it
to a single device, specify the COM port.
In this example the command is targeted at `b1-display`, so it will only apply
to this module type.

content.content:
# Framework Laptop 16 Input Modules Repository

## What is this repo?

This is an open-source documentation and reference design repository for Framework Laptop 16's modular input system. As described, it contains "mechanical and electrical documentation for the Input Module system" that enables customization of the laptop's keyboard, touchpad, and other input devices.

The repository includes:
- **Mechanical designs** for three module sizes (keyboard, numpad, and half-sized)
- **Electrical schematics** (KiCAD reference designs based on RP2040)
- **Firmware references** linking to QMK and Rust-based implementations
- **Protocol documentation** for USB 2.0, I2C, and HID interfaces

## Installation/Build Requirements

**No sudo or complex installation needed.** This is a documentation repository, not executable software. Users can:

1. **Access designs directly**: View 2D mechanical drawings and KiCAD electrical files from the folders
2. **Reference firmware separately**: Build instructions exist in linked repositories (QMK firmware, inputmodule-rs) with their own build processes
3. **Develop custom modules**: Use the pinout specifications and electrical interface documentation to create compatible devices

The repository itself requires no compilation or privileged access—it's purely reference material licensed under CC BY 4.0.

[target.'cfg(all(target_arch = "arm", target_os = "none"))']
# Choose a default "cargo run" tool:
# - probe-run provides flashing and defmt via a hardware debugger
# - elf2uf2-rs loads firmware over USB when the rp2040 is in boot mode
# runner = "probe-run --chip RP2040"
runner = "elf2uf2-rs -d"

rustflags = [
  "-C", "linker=flip-link",
  "-C", "link-arg=--nmagic",
  "-C", "link-arg=-Tlink.x",
  "-C", "link-arg=-Tdefmt.x",

  # Code-size optimizations.
  #   trap unreachable can save a lot of space, but requires nightly compiler.
  #   uncomment the next line if you wish to enable it
  # "-Z", "trap-unreachable=no",
  "-C", "llvm-args=--inline-threshold=5",
  "-C", "no-vectorize-loops",
]

[build]
target = "thumbv6m-none-eabi"

[env]
DEFMT_LOG = "debug"

# Claude Web vs Claude Code (Desktop) — What's Different

This document explains the key differences between **Claude on the web** (claude.ai) and **Claude Code** (the desktop/terminal app) so you know what to expect from each.

---

## Quick Summary

| Feature | Claude Web (claude.ai) | Claude Code (Desktop) |
|---|---|---|
| Skills / Custom Instructions | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your machine |
| Memory | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your machine |
| Syncs across devices | Yes | No |
| Access to your local files | No | Yes |
| Run terminal commands | No | Yes |
| Works offline | No | Partially |
| Hooks / Automation | No | Yes |
| MCP Server integrations | Limited | Yes |

---

## The Big One: Nothing Syncs Between Web and Desktop

**Skills, memory, and settings do not sync between claude.ai and Claude Code.**

If you create a skill or custom instruction on claude.ai, it will not appear in Claude Code — and vice versa. They are completely separate systems.

---

## Skills / Custom Instructions

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Skills and custom instructions are saved to your Anthropic account.
- They are available on any device when you log in to claude.ai.
- You manage them through the claude.ai interface.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Skills are stored as local files on your computer.
  - **Linux/Mac:** `~/.claude/skills/`
  - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\`
- They are only available on the machine where they were created.
- They do not follow your account — if you log in on a different computer, your skills will not be there unless you copy the files.

---

## Memory

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude's memory of you and your preferences is tied to your account.
- It is available across all devices where you are logged in.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Memory is stored in local files on your computer.
  - **Linux/Mac:** `~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/`
  - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\projects\<project>\memory\`
- Memory is per-machine and per-project. It does not sync to other devices or to claude.ai.

---

## File and System Access

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude cannot access files on your computer unless you manually upload them.
- Claude cannot run commands on your machine.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Claude can read and write files on your computer directly.
- Claude can run terminal/shell commands on your machine.
- This is one of the main reasons to use Claude Code over the web version.

---

## Conversations

Conversations are not shared between the two products. A conversation started on claude.ai will not appear in Claude Code, and vice versa.

---

## Settings and Automation (Hooks)

Hooks (automated actions that run before/after Claude does something) are a Claude Code-only feature. They are configured locally and have no equivalent on claude.ai.

---

## When to Use Which

| Use Case | Recommended |
|---|---|
| Quick questions, general chat | Either |
| Working on code or files on your computer | Claude Code |
| Needs to run on multiple devices / machines | Claude Web |
| Automated workflows and scripts | Claude Code |
| Sharing a setup with a team (same machine) | Claude Code with shared skill files |

---

## Common Confusion Points

**"My skills are missing on my other computer."**
Claude Code skills are local files. Copy the `~/.claude/skills/` folder to the other machine.

**"My skills from the website don't work in Claude Code."**
Web skills and Claude Code skills are separate. You need to recreate them as local files in Claude Code.

**"Claude doesn't remember our conversation from last time."**
Check which product you used last time. Memory does not sync between web and desktop, and Claude Code memory is per-project and per-machine.

**"Claude Code isn't doing what it does on the website."**
Claude Code may have different skills, memory, or settings configured locally. The two products can behave differently based on their local vs. account-based configurations.

# Claude Web vs Claude Code (Desktop) — What's Different

This document explains the key differences between **Claude on the web** (claude.ai) and **Claude Code** (the desktop/terminal app) so you know what to expect from each.

---

## Quick Summary

| Feature | Claude Web (claude.ai) | Claude Code (Desktop) |
|---|---|---|
| Skills / Custom Instructions | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your machine |
| Memory | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your machine |
| Syncs across devices | Yes | No |
| Access to your local files | No | Yes |
| Run terminal commands | No | Yes |
| Works offline | No | Partially |
| Hooks / Automation | No | Yes |
| MCP Server integrations | Limited | Yes |

---

## The Big One: Nothing Syncs Between Web and Desktop

**Skills, memory, and settings do not sync between claude.ai and Claude Code.**

If you create a skill or custom instruction on claude.ai, it will not appear in Claude Code — and vice versa. They are completely separate systems.

---

## Skills / Custom Instructions

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Skills and custom instructions are saved to your Anthropic account.
- They are available on any device when you log in to claude.ai.
- You manage them through the claude.ai interface.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Skills are stored as local files on your computer.
  - **Linux/Mac:** `~/.claude/skills/`
  - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\`
- They are only available on the machine where they were created.
- They do not follow your account — if you log in on a different computer, your skills will not be there unless you copy the files.

---

## Memory

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude's memory of you and your preferences is tied to your account.
- It is available across all devices where you are logged in.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Memory is stored in local files on your computer.
  - **Linux/Mac:** `~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/`
  - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\projects\<project>\memory\`
- Memory is per-machine and per-project. It does not sync to other devices or to claude.ai.

---

## File and System Access

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude cannot access files on your computer unless you manually upload them.
- Claude cannot run commands on your machine.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Claude can read and write files on your computer directly.
- Claude can run terminal/shell commands on your machine.
- This is one of the main reasons to use Claude Code over the web version.

---

## Conversations

Conversations are not shared between the two products. A conversation started on claude.ai will not appear in Claude Code, and vice versa.

---

## Settings and Automation (Hooks)

Hooks (automated actions that run before/after Claude does something) are a Claude Code-only feature. They are configured locally and have no equivalent on claude.ai.

---

## When to Use Which

| Use Case | Recommended |
|---|---|
| Quick questions, general chat | Either |
| Working on code or files on your computer | Claude Code |
| Needs to run on multiple devices / machines | Claude Web |
| Automated workflows and scripts | Claude Code |
| Sharing a setup with a team (same machine) | Claude Code with shared skill files |

---

## Common Confusion Points

**"My skills are missing on my other computer."**
Claude Code skills are local files. Copy the `~/.claude/skills/` folder to the other machine.

**"My skills from the website don't work in Claude Code."**
Web skills and Claude Code skills are separate. You need to recreate them as local files in Claude Code.

**"Claude doesn't remember our conversation from last time."**
Check which product you used last time. Memory does not sync between web and desktop, and Claude Code memory is per-project and per-machine.

**"Claude Code isn't doing what it does on the website."**
Claude Code may have different skills, memory, or settings configured locally. The two products can behave differently based on their local vs. account-based configurations.

input.content:
# Claude Web vs Claude Code (Desktop) — What's Different

This document explains the key differences between **Claude on the web** (claude.ai) and **Claude Code** (the desktop app) so you know what to expect from each.

---

## Quick Summary

| Feature | Claude Web (claude.ai) | Claude Code (Desktop) |
|---|---|---|
| Skills / Custom Instructions | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your PC |
| Memory | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your PC |
| Syncs across devices | Yes | No |
| Access to your local files | No | Yes |
| Run commands on your PC | No | Yes |
| Works offline | No | Partially |
| Looks like | A website in your browser | A terminal/command-line window |
| Login | Your Anthropic account | Same Anthropic account |
| Hooks / Automation | No | Yes |

---

## Important: These Are Two Separate Products

Claude Web and Claude Code share the same AI, but they are **two separate products** that do not talk to each other.

- They use the same Anthropic account to log in.
- Skills, memory, settings, and conversations **do not sync** between them.
- What you set up in one will **not** appear in the other.

---

## What Claude Code Looks Like

Claude Code does **not** look like a typical Windows application. It opens as a **terminal window** (similar to Command Prompt or PowerShell). This is expected — it is designed for working directly with files and code on your computer.

If a user opens Claude Code and sees a black/text window, that is correct.

---

## Skills / Custom Instructions

Skills are saved responses, behaviors, or instructions you give Claude to follow.

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Skills are saved to your Anthropic account online.
- Available on any device — phone, tablet, another PC — as long as you are logged in.
- Managed through the claude.ai website settings.

### Claude Code (Desktop — Windows)
- Skills are saved as files on your local PC.
- Location: `C:\Users\<YourUsername>\AppData\Roaming\Claude\skills\`
- To open this folder in File Explorer, paste this into the address bar: `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\`
- Skills are **only on the PC where they were created**.
- Logging into a different PC will not bring your skills with you — you would need to copy the files manually.

---

## Memory

Memory is Claude's ability to remember things about you, your preferences, or your projects over time.

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Memory is tied to your account and stored online.
- Available across all devices where you are logged in.

### Claude Code (Desktop — Windows)
- Memory is saved as files on your local PC.
- Location: `C:\Users\<YourUsername>\AppData\Roaming\Claude\projects\<project name>\memory\`
- Memory is specific to each PC and each project folder.
- It does **not** sync to other PCs or to claude.ai.

---

## File and System Access

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude cannot see or access files on your PC unless you upload them manually.
- Claude cannot run programs or commands on your machine.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Claude can read, write, and edit files on your PC directly.
- Claude can run commands in the terminal on your machine.
- This makes Claude Code much more powerful for tasks involving your local files and projects.

---

## Internet Connection

Both products require an internet connection to work. Claude Code is a desktop application, but it still connects to Anthropic's servers to generate responses. It is **not** fully offline.

---

## Conversations

Conversations are not shared between the two products. A conversation started on claude.ai will not appear in Claude Code, and vice versa. Each product keeps its own separate conversation history.

---

## Settings and Automation (Hooks)

Hooks are automated actions — for example, running a task automatically whenever Claude finishes a response. This is a **Claude Code-only** feature and has no equivalent on claude.ai.

---

## When to Use Which

| Use Case | Recommended |
|---|---|
| Quick questions, general chat | Either |
| Working with files or code on your PC | Claude Code |
| Needs to work across multiple devices | Claude Web |
| Automated workflows tied to your PC | Claude Code |
| Users who are not comfortable with a terminal window | Claude Web |

---

## Common Confusion Points

**"My skills are missing on my other computer."**
Claude Code skills are stored only on the PC where they were created. To move them, copy the folder `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\` from the old PC to the same location on the new PC.

**"My skills from the website don't show up in Claude Code."**
Web skills and Claude Code skills are completely separate. Skills created on claude.ai do not transfer to Claude Code. They would need to be recreated locally.

**"Claude doesn't remember anything from last time."**
Check which product was used last time. Memory does not sync between web and desktop. Claude Code memory is also specific to each PC and each project folder.

**"Claude Code looks different from the website."**
Claude Code is a terminal application — it will look and feel different from claude.ai. It may also behave differently because it has its own local skills, memory, and settings that are separate from the web version.

**"I can't find the skills or memory folder."**
Open File Explorer and type `%APPDATA%\Claude\` in the address bar and press Enter. This will take you directly to Claude Code's local data folder on your PC.

**"Does Claude Code cost extra?"**
No. Claude Code uses the same Anthropic account and subscription as claude.ai. You do not need a separate subscription.

If a user opens Claude Code and sees a black/text window, that is correct.

---

## Skills / Custom Instructions

Skills are saved responses, behaviors, or instructions you give Claude to follow.

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Skills are saved to your Anthropic account online.
- Available on any device — phone, tablet, another PC — as long as you are logged in.
- Managed through the claude.ai website settings.

### Claude Code (Desktop — Windows)
- Skills are saved as files on your local PC.
- Location: `C:\Users\<YourUsername>\AppData\Roaming\Claude\skills\`
- To open this folder in File Explorer, paste this into the address bar: `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\`
- Skills are **only on the PC where they were created**.
- Logging into a different PC will not bring your skills with you — you would need to copy the files manually.

---

## Memory

Memory is Claude's ability to remember things about you, your preferences, or your projects over time.

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Memory is tied to your account and stored online.
- Available across all devices where you are logged in.

### Claude Code (Desktop — Windows)
- Memory is saved as files on your local PC.
- Location: `C:\Users\<YourUsername>\AppData\Roaming\Claude\projects\<project name>\memory\`
- Memory is specific to each PC and each project folder.
- It does **not** sync to other PCs or to claude.ai.

---

## File and System Access

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude cannot see or access files on your PC unless you upload them manually.
- Claude cannot run programs or commands on your machine.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Claude can read, write, and edit files on your PC directly.
- Claude can run commands in the terminal on your machine.
- This makes Claude Code much more powerful for tasks involving your local files and projects.

---

## Internet Connection

Both products require an internet connection to work. Claude Code is a desktop application, but it still connects to Anthropic's servers to generate responses. It is **not** fully offline.

---

## Conversations

Conversations are not shared between the two products. A conversation started on claude.ai will not appear in Claude Code, and vice versa. Each product keeps its own separate conversation history.

---

## Settings and Automation (Hooks)

Hooks are automated actions — for example, running a task automatically whenever Claude finishes a response. This is a **Claude Code-only** feature and has no equivalent on claude.ai.

---

## When to Use Which

| Use Case | Recommended |
|---|---|
| Quick questions, general chat | Either |
| Working with files or code on your PC | Claude Code |
| Needs to work across multiple devices | Claude Web |
| Automated workflows tied to your PC | Claude Code |
| Users who are not comfortable with a terminal window | Claude Web |

---

## Common Confusion Points

**"My skills are missing on my other computer."**
Claude Code skills are stored only on the PC where they were created. To move them, copy the folder `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\` from the old PC to the same location on the new PC.

**"My skills from the website don't show up in Claude Code."**
Web skills and Claude Code skills are completely separate. Skills created on claude.ai do not transfer to Claude Code. They would need to be recreated locally.

**"Claude doesn't remember anything from last time."**
Check which product was used last time. Memory does not sync between web and desktop. Claude Code memory is also specific to each PC and each project folder.

**"Claude Code looks different from the website."**
Claude Code is a terminal application — it will look and feel different from claude.ai. It may also behave differently because it has its own local skills, memory, and settings that are separate from the web version.

**"I can't find the skills or memory folder."**
Open File Explorer and type `%APPDATA%\Claude\` in the address bar and press Enter. This will take you directly to Claude Code's local data folder on your PC.

**"Does Claude Code cost extra?"**
No. Claude Code uses the same Anthropic account and subscription as claude.ai. You do not need a separate subscription.

# Claude Web vs Claude Code (Desktop) — What's Different

This document explains the key differences between **Claude on the web** (claude.ai) and **Claude Code** (the desktop/terminal app) so you know what to expect from each.

---

## Quick Summary

| Feature | Claude Web (claude.ai) | Claude Code (Desktop) |
|---|---|---|
| Skills / Custom Instructions | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your machine |
| Memory | Stored on Anthropic's servers | Stored locally on your machine |
| Syncs across devices | Yes | No |
| Access to your local files | No | Yes |
| Run terminal commands | No | Yes |
| Works offline | No | Partially |
| Hooks / Automation | No | Yes |
| MCP Server integrations | Limited | Yes |

---

## The Big One: Nothing Syncs Between Web and Desktop

**Skills, memory, and settings do not sync between claude.ai and Claude Code.**

If you create a skill or custom instruction on claude.ai, it will not appear in Claude Code — and vice versa. They are completely separate systems.

---

## Skills / Custom Instructions

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Skills and custom instructions are saved to your Anthropic account.
- They are available on any device when you log in to claude.ai.
- You manage them through the claude.ai interface.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Skills are stored as local files on your computer.
  - **Linux/Mac:** `~/.claude/skills/`
  - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\skills\`
- They are only available on the machine where they were created.
- They do not follow your account — if you log in on a different computer, your skills will not be there unless you copy the files.

---

## Memory

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude's memory of you and your preferences is tied to your account.
- It is available across all devices where you are logged in.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Memory is stored in local files on your computer.
  - **Linux/Mac:** `~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/`
  - **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\projects\<project>\memory\`
- Memory is per-machine and per-project. It does not sync to other devices or to claude.ai.

---

## File and System Access

### Claude Web (claude.ai)
- Claude cannot access files on your computer unless you manually upload them.
- Claude cannot run commands on your machine.

### Claude Code (Desktop)
- Claude can read and write files on your computer directly.
- Claude can run terminal/shell commands on your machine.
- This is one of the main reasons to use Claude Code over the web version.

---

## Conversations

Conversations are not shared between the two products. A conversation started on claude.ai will not appear in Claude Code, and vice versa.

---

## Settings and Automation (Hooks)

Hooks (automated actions that run before/after Claude does something) are a Claude Code-only feature. They are configured locally and have no equivalent on claude.ai.

---

## When to Use Which

| Use Case | Recommended |
|---|---|
| Quick questions, general chat | Either |
| Working on code or files on your computer | Claude Code |
| Needs to run on multiple devices / machines | Claude Web |
| Automated workflows and scripts | Claude Code |
| Sharing a setup with a team (same machine) | Claude Code with shared skill files |

---

## Common Confusion Points

**"My skills are missing on my other computer."**
Claude Code skills are local files. Copy the `~/.claude/skills/` folder to the other machine.

**"My skills from the website don't work in Claude Code."**
Web skills and Claude Code skills are separate. You need to recreate them as local files in Claude Code.

**"Claude doesn't remember our conversation from last time."**
Check which product you used last time. Memory does not sync between web and desktop, and Claude Code memory is per-project and per-machine.

**"Claude Code isn't doing what it does on the website."**
Claude Code may have different skills, memory, or settings configured locally. The two products can behave differently based on their local vs. account-based configurations.
